observations.recommendation:
1.13. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant prepare an adjusting journal entry for the
unreleased/unclaimed checks issued and the stale checks to restore
the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account and the liabilities
account in the amount of ₱1,216,441.48 and ₱23,426.54, respectively,
to faithfully present the financial position of the Municipality as at
December 31, 2024. Moving forward, the Municipal Treasurer to
submit to the Municipal Accounting Office at each reporting period a
report on the unreleased/unclaimed checks for the proper recording
by the latter of the said transaction in the books of accounts.

2.12. We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts
and ensure that the basis of the recognition of the Real Property Tax
and Special Education Tax Receivables accounts and the
corresponding deferred accounts be based on the Certified List of Tax
Payers and Taxes Due as prepared by the Municipal Treasury Office,
in accordance with provisions of Section 20, Chapter 3 of the New
Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local
Government Units (LGUs), Volume 1 and Paragraph 59, International
Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 23.

3.14. We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant facilitate the reversion to the unappropriated surplus of
the General Fund, the accounts payable in the amount of ₱61,823.33,
which has been outstanding for two years or more and against which
no claim, administrative or judicial, has been filed or which is not
covered by perfected contracts on record in accordance with
International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 41 and
Section 98 of Presidential Decree 1445.

4.14 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Appraisal
Committee determine the valuation of the donated property
consisting of the generator set and the laptop and furnish the said
report as basis of the Municipal Accountant in recording the donated
items in accordance with International Public Sector Accounting
Standards (IPSAS) No. 17 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated
December 1, 2015, with consideration on the capitalization threshold
as prescribed under COA Circular 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022.

5.12 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant act on the request for authority to write-off unliquidated
cash advances and other receivable accounts or the application of the
proper disposition and derecognition of dormant accounts,
particularly those accounts which were no longer traceable and
whose accountable officers were already deceased, in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

6.7 We recommended and the Management agreed that:
6.7.1 The Municipal Agriculturist submits to the Municipal Accountant
an inventory report containing the complete details of all
breeding stocks, including quantity, age, valuation, among
others, as the basis for the latter to make the necessary
adjustments to reflect the accurate balance of the Breeding
Stocks account in the Statement of Financial Position.
6.7.2 The Accountable Officer, in this case, the Municipal
Agriculturist, files his application for relief from accountability in
compliance with COA Circular No. 92-386 dated
October 20, 1992, and COA Memorandum No. 92-751 dated
February 24, 1992.

7.9. We recommended and the Management agreed to create a committee
to formulate a policy/guideline on the grant of financial assistance
clearly stating therein the eligibility criteria for the grant, the required
documents to support the claim, and the amount and nature of the
grant to ensure fair and equitable implementation of the program in
line with the basic policy of the State of ensuring efficient, economical
and effective utilization of government resources as prescribed under
Section 2 of Presidential Decree 1445.

8.10. We recommended and the Management agreed that together with the
Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office, maximize
the utilization of the current year’s Prevention and Mitigation Fund
and the continuing appropriations of the LDRRMF for the
implementation of disaster management programs, projects and
activities in the Municipality to ensure the attainment of the objectives
of Republic Act 10121, the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management (PDRRM) Act of 2010.

9.8. We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local School
Board (LSB) prepare, submit, and post the Quarterly and Annual SEF
Utilization reports within the prescribed period to ensure compliance
with Item 6.1 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated
January 19, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant revisit the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units and ensure that appropriate accounts are used in the recording of transactions in the books of the Municipality, as prescribed in Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the transfer the completed project amounting ₱396,213.60 from the Trust Fund to the General Fund in consonance to the provisions of Paragraph 7 of International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1 and Section 104.1.i of COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to properly record the balances of the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables accounts and the corresponding deferred accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the valuation of the donated lot at ₱100,000.00, in consonance with Item 27 of International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 17.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Budget Officer, being the assigned personnel, submit the paid disbursement vouchers on the grant of cash advance for Confidential Fund and its required supporting documents to the concerned COA Audit Team Leader within seven (7) days from release of check in compliance with the COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular              No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
The Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) based on the standard format as prescribed in Appendix 60 of the Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Unit (GAM for LGU)-Volume 2.
The Municipal Accountant and the MGSO maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, for each item of inventory in stock for check and balance and reconciliation to support the balance of the Inventories reflected in the financial statements in adherence to Section 114, Volume I, of COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Assessor exert efforts in the processing of the pertinent Certificate of Title of the untitled lots of the Municipality in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the Municipality pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and
The Municipality allocate sufficient funds to cover the expenses pertinent to the titling of government lots in accordance with the applicable budgeting and accounting rules and regulations.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) adopt ways in reducing the delays in the Award Process, particularly the issuance of the Notice of Award, the perfection of the Contract and the issuance of the Notice to Proceed to facilitate the immediate implementation of the Municipality’s priority projects.
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accounting and the Treasury Offices adopt a mechanism to ensure proper monitoring and submission of financial transaction documents.

The Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts and ensure that the basis of the recognition of the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables accounts at the beginning of the year should be the Certified List of Tax Payers and Taxes Due as prepared by the Municipal Treasury Office, in compliance with the provisions of Section 20, Chapter 3 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I and Paragraph 59, International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 23.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Management to:
Make representation from COA Central Office to follow-up the status of Liquidation Reports for the cash advances pertaining to Confidential Fund in calendar years 2012 to 2014.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to continually issue written notices/demand letters to the concerned accountable officer to require the immediate settlement thereof pursuant to COA Circular                      No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal General Services Officer to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, for each item of inventory in stock for check and balance and reconciliation to support the balance of the Inventories reflected in the financial statements in adherence to Section 114, Volume I, of COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated      June 20, 2002.
Consider increasing the number of personnel in the General Services Office to address the manpower constraints and to facilitate the maintenance of the required records and reports.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the distributed breeding stocks and to submit to COA the related journal entry vouchers;
Facilitate the application of request for relief from property accountability for the loss of breeding stocks due to Typhoon Odette to ensure filing of the request within the prescribed period, in compliance with Section 151 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to ensure adequate disclosures in the notes to the financial statements on the impairments of the related breeding stocks; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the provisions of International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 27 by updating the breeding stocks balance and record the additions and reductions at every reporting period in order to ensure fair presentation of the Biological Asset account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the heads of the Human Resource Office and Municipal Health Office to ensure the correctness of the computations on the claims for Active Hazard Duty Pay (AHDP), Special Risk Allowance (SRA) and such other eligible benefits, in compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations.
Require the refund by the concerned personnel of the excess grant of AHDP and SRA in the amount of ₱34,909.50.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Local Finance Committee to:
Prepare the required quarterly reports on fund utilization and status of program/project implementation of Local Government Support Fund – Financial Assistance using the prescribed format (Annex C) of the Local Budget Circular No. 131 dated               July 27, 2021;
Ensure that the financial information is made available to the public, in adherence to the prescribed guidelines; and
Send a written notice to the Secretary of DBM, Secretary of DILG, Speaker of the House of Representatives, President of the Senate of the Philippines, and the Chairpersons of the House Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance upon posting. Also, submit to the Office of the Auditor copy of the said written notice.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require all offices concerned, such as the end-users and the Procurement Unit of the Municipality, not to specify brand names in its Purchase Request and the related procurement documents instead, state the specifications based on relevant characteristics, functionality and performance requirements in compliance to the provisions of Section 18 of the Republic Act 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations; and
Require the BAC to observe the posting requirements in the PhilGEPS website, agency website/page, and/or at any conspicuous place of the locality pursuant to the provisions of Section 54 of the same Act and its Revised IRR.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Bids and Awards Committee and its secretariat as well as the Municipal General Services Officer and other personnel concerned to submit to COA all perfected/issued contracts and Purchase Orders within five (5) working days from its perfection/issuance, consistent with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009; and
Ensure that Purchase Order on all procurement of the Municipality are properly accomplished, in consonance to the requirements of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 to facilitate imposition of liquidated damages and other indemnification on late deliveries, if any.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents in accordance with Section 100 of Presidential Decree 1445 and Section 7.2.1.a of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and henceforth, to strictly follow the regulations in the submission of accounts; and
Direct the Accounting and the Treasury Offices to adopt a mechanism to ensure proper monitoring and submission of financial transaction documents.

We recommended and Management agreed to limit the payroll deductions to the order of preference provided in the GAA and ensure that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed take home pay of ₱5,000.00 as provided under Section 49 of Republic Act 11518 General Appropriations Act of FY 2021.

Stop the practice of procuring goods and expenses not related to COVID-19 instead, spend the amounts to PPAs that can help strengthen the prevention and mitigation measures; and

Adhere strictly to the provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125 in the utilization of the BGCM Fund of the Municipality.
